author: codex
scope: validator/rule-data triage only for next relation endpoint bucket after next5/relation024; artifact-only prep; no provider-backed post-ingest commands; no generated-output, override, journal, LOG, manifest, wiki, or code edits

recommendation.summary: Do not widen has_timeline_event to accept claim subjects. After excluding the next4 has_metric claim patch, relation023 treatment timeline cleanup rows, and relation024 implemented_by treatment cleanup rows, the largest remaining endpoint-shape bucket is five claim-owned timeline edges. These rows use claims as timeline carriers for board votes, implementation dates, committee-agenda updates, or schedule-change context. That matches the batch020 precedent where similar claim -> event timeline links were treated as data cleanup and replaced with looser event -> claim dependency/reference edges.
recommendation.recommended_action: Prepare a narrow retire-and-replace data cleanup batch for the five malformed has_timeline_event claim -> event relations. Prefer supports event -> claim replacements, preserving the same source-backed event and claim endpoints; for rows with stronger meeting/agenda semantics, has_claim event -> claim is a legal alternative, but this should be decided row-by-row in the cleanup prep rather than hidden behind a broad validator rule.

do_not_patch_validator_yet.reason: Claims are source-stated assertions, not durable timeline owners. The remaining examples are mixed and often express contextual support, agenda membership, or missing project/source carrier modeling. Widening has_timeline_event for claim subjects would bless weak claim-owned chronology and regress against batch020's data-cleanup treatment of the same endpoint shape.

excluded_from_this_triage.reason: next4 has_metric claim -> metric_claim validator patch

has_timeline_event_rule.current_rejection_message: Relation kind has_timeline_event expects project|route|corridor|entity|source|event -> event, got claim -> event.

precedent.decision: Retire four has_timeline_event claim -> event rows and replace them with supports event -> claim rows.

targeted_inventory_after_exclusions.method: Read-only bun --eval over readCanonicalRecords() and relationEndpointShapeIssue(); excluded relation023 and relation024 original record ids; did not run CLI validate, materialize, export-jsonl, DB repair/publish, or provider-backed commands.

top_shape_buckets.classification: defer; metric claims are also poor timeline owners, but this review keeps the next bucket narrow
defer; likely agency-role/relation-kind cleanup, not validator widening
defer; likely agency-role/relation-kind cleanup, not validator widening
defer; publication role should probably attach source/project/entity carriers, not claim subjects
defer; source object suggests containment/reference cleanup
defer; likely endpoint identity or relation-kind cleanup

simulated_impact.assumption: All five original malformed relations are retired and validator-compatible replacements are submitted/materialized. This artifact did not perform createSubmissionEntry dry-runs for the five replacements and did not edit retired.json or submissions journals.
simulated_impact.why_not_validator_impact: A validator patch allowing claim -> event would also reduce the count by five, but it would validate weak claim-owned timeline modeling and contradict the existing batch020 cleanup precedent.

subject.claim_text: Strengthen & Expand the Network - Board to Vote on Tolling Structure for Congestion Pricing

object.display_name: Board Vote on Congestion Pricing Tolling Structure

evidence.text: Board to Vote on Tolling Structure for Congestion Pricing

exact_issue_examples.cleanup_read: The board vote event is contextual support for a presentation theme/claim. Replace with supports event -> claim, or has_claim event -> claim if the cleanup owner wants a stronger event-owned claim edge.

subject.claim_text: Provide frequency increases and/or running time adjustments on eight express bus routes and 16 local bus routes/route combinations

object.display_name: Express Bus Service Enhancements Implementation - Spring 2025

evidence.text: Provide frequency increases and/or running time adjustments on eight express bus routes and 16 local bus routes/route combinations.

exact_issue_examples.cleanup_read: The implementation date is a timeline detail for service enhancements, but the source has no project carrier in current canonical records. Preserve context as supports event -> claim rather than admitting claim as a has_timeline_event owner.

subject.claim_text: Provide frequency increases and/or running time adjustments on eight express bus routes and 16 local bus routes/route combinations

object.display_name: Local Bus Service Enhancements Implementation - Summer 2025

evidence.text: Provide frequency increases and/or running time adjustments on eight express bus routes and 16 local bus routes/route combinations.

exact_issue_examples.cleanup_read: Same source and subject as the express row. A supports event -> claim replacement keeps the schedule context without changing the timeline-owner rule.

subject.claim_text: The proposed adjustments are expected to be cost neutral.

evidence.text: The proposed adjustments are expected to be cost neutral.
These proposed minor subway schedule changes would be implemented in May 2026.

exact_issue_examples.cleanup_read: This is weaker than a clean claim-event dependency: both records describe the same proposed schedule-change package, but the May implementation event does not specifically support the cost-neutral claim. Prefer source has_timeline_event source -> event plus a separate source/claim context if available, or use supports event -> claim only with a medium-confidence caveat.

subject.claim_text: The committee will receive an update on the status of Recommendations issued to the MTA by the NTSB.

evidence.text: The committee will be briefed on commuter railroad Safety Highlights.
The committee will receive an update on the status of Recommendations issued to the MTA by the NTSB.

exact_issue_examples.cleanup_read: The relation label/evidence currently points at Railroad Safety Highlights while the subject claim is NTSB Recommendations Update. Retire this row; if replacing, use the NTSB evidence refs and a supports or has_claim event -> claim edge from the November committee meeting to the NTSB claim.

guards_for_followup_cleanup_prep: Re-run the targeted inventory against the then-current canonical DB because relation024 is applied durably but may still be pending generated-output checkpoint.
For each original relation, verify the original submission id is not already retired and no prior relation cleanup batch has already replaced it.
Use createSubmissionEntry plus relationEndpointIssues in dry-run/local validation mode for any proposed replacement entries before editing retired.json or submissions journals.
For meeting_doc_199141, correct the evidence to NTSB refs if replacing the NTSB claim edge; do not preserve the Railroad Safety Highlights mismatch.
Do not include has_timeline_event metric_claim -> event or any agency/program/route buckets in the same cleanup batch.
commands_run: bun --eval '<targeted endpoint-shape inventory over readCanonicalRecords() and relationEndpointShapeIssue(), excluding relation023 and relation024 original record ids>'
rg -n "claim->event|claim -> event|has_timeline_event claim|metric_claim->event|metric_claim -> event|timeline.*claim" data/review_notes docs packages -S
bun --eval '<dump all remaining has_timeline_event claim->event rows with relation, subject, object, evidence_refs, and current issue message>'

for ref in meeting_doc_160441#p001_c0012 meeting_doc_160441#p005_c0005 meeting_doc_160441#p001_c0013 meeting_doc_160441#p005_c0006 meeting_doc_160441#p001_c0007 meeting_doc_160441#p004_c0006 meeting_doc_196866#p001_c0009 meeting_doc_196866#p001_c0011 meeting_doc_199141#p002_c0023 meeting_doc_199141#p002_c0024 meeting_doc_199141#p002_c0025 meeting_doc_199141#p002_c0026 meeting_doc_135731#p002_c0002 meeting_doc_135731#p002_c0004; do ... jq raw block text ...; done
bun --eval '<list same-source canonical records for meeting_doc_135731, meeting_doc_160441, meeting_doc_196866, and meeting_doc_199141>'

bun --eval 'import { relationEndpointShapeIssue } from "./packages/pipeline/src/records/relations.ts"; console.log(JSON.stringify({has_timeline_claim_event: relationEndpointShapeIssue("has_timeline_event", "claim", "event")?.message, supports_event_claim: relationEndpointShapeIssue("supports", "event", "claim") ?? null, has_claim_event_claim: relationEndpointShapeIssue("has_claim", "event", "claim") ?? null, has_timeline_source_event: relationEndpointShapeIssue("has_timeline_event", "source", "event") ?? null}, null, 2));'
